zotero.abstractNote: In this paper, we propose a hierarchical decomposition of nonlinear dynamics and control. We decompose the system dynamics into a linear time-invariant (LTI) base model and a nonlinear residual model. This decomposition allows us to use well-established linear control techniques for the base model and to learn the residual dynamics using Gaussian processes. We show that this approach leads to a more sample-efficient system identification and allows for a hierarchical policy distillation, where a complex policy is distilled into a simpler, more interpretable one. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on several simulated and real-world robotic systems.
zotero.conferenceName: Conference on Learning for Dynamics and Control

extra.Editors: Alexandre M. Bayen and Ali Jadbabaie and George Pappas and Pablo A. Parrilo and Benjamin Recht and Claire Tomlin and Melanie Zeilinger

zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 2nd Conference on Learning for Dynamics and Control
